text: Following the establishment of pneumoperitoneum on the right flank, the surgeon inserts two operative trochars: a five-millimeter trochar in the right iliac fossa and a ten-millimeter trochar at the epigastrium.
The surgeon frees adhesions circumferentially, carefully separating the adhered tissues to improve access to the surgical site.
The surgeon cuts a DynaMesh IPOM mesh and inserts it through the ten-millimeter trochar located at the epigastrium.
The slit in the surgical field surrounds the bowel, indicating the positioning of the bowel relative to the incision.
The surgeon secures the DynaMesh IPOM mesh to the abdominal wall using both absorbable and non-absorbable tacks, ensuring stable fixation of the mesh within the surgical field.
The surgeon closes the overlapping cut margins of the DynaMesh IPOM mesh with two stitches, securing the mesh in place within the abdominal cavity.
The DynaMesh IPOM mesh visibly wraps around the bowel as it transitions from the abdominal cavity into the abdominal wall.
The surgeon creates a ten to twelve centimeter route for the end column using interruptive sutures, instead of employing a second mesh to cover the bowel as in the sandwich technique.
The surgeon uses PDS-polydeoxanon-trio sutures to fix the edges of the bowel serosa to the abdominal wall, aiming to reduce the risk of prolapse hernia over time.
